_meta.description: Azure Monitor scheduled query rule that fires Sev1 whenever the W297 risk lifecycle counter `risk.plan_review.audit.verified` reports `result=broken` more than 0 times in any 15-minute window. A broken hash chain in plan-review audit indicates either tampering or a chain-rotation bug — both PDPL-reportable.
depends_on: W299 Log Analytics workbook (risk_metrics_CL custom table)
W302 Prometheus /metrics → if scraped into Azure Monitor managed Prometheus, alternate query lives in `_promql` field

placeholders.<resource-group>: Resource group hosting the Log Analytics workspace
placeholders.<action-group-resource-id>: Resource ID of the Saudi on-call action group (PagerDuty / Teams / SMS to MFA-tier-2 admins)

properties.description: Sev1: the hash-linked audit chain on a triggered plan-review is broken. Indicates tampering or registry rotation bug. PDPL Article 11 — incident reporting required.

customProperties.ar_summary: كَسر في سلسلة تدقيق مراجعة خطة الرعاية — تحقيق فوري مطلوب

_promql_alternate._note: If running Azure Monitor managed Prometheus (W302 /metrics scrape), use this alert rule instead of the KQL-based scheduledQueryRule above.

annotations.summary: Plan-review audit chain broken in last 15m
annotations.description: {{ $value }} broken-chain verifications on {{ $labels.instance }} — investigate tampering or registry rotation bug.
